data.title: James Scott Farrin Partner Walter Wood Named to "South Carolina Lawyers Weekly" 2026 'Power List' for Personal Injury*
data.description: In Greenville, South Carolina, on March 5, 2026, the Law Offices of James Scott Farrin expressed pride in Partner Walter McBrayer Wood for his selection on the reputable 2026 list. Wood's contribution to advancing personal injury law in South Carolina has been recognized, highlighting his significant role in the field.

data.title: Costco Earnings Due. These Retailers Retreat On Results; One Surges.
data.description: The stock of Costco is approaching its entry point leading up to its Thursday earnings report. Meanwhile, Victoria's Secret and BJ's have experienced a decline in their stock value. Conversely, Burlington's stock has surpassed its buy zone. The upcoming Costco earnings report is significant for investors. This news comes amidst contrasting performances in the retail sector, with some companies tumbling and one experiencing a surge in stock value.

data.title: Tango Therapeutics, Up 39% This Year, Surges Again On Another Cancer Pact
data.description: Tango Therapeutics, one of the top 1% biotech stocks, experienced a significant surge in shares on Thursday following an agreement to codevelop a cancer treatment with Erasca. The company, whose shares have risen by 39% this year, saw another notable increase.

data.title: Oracle Reportedly Planning To Cut Thousands Of Jobs
data.description: Shares of Oracle saw a small increase after news surfaced that the company is preparing to slash thousands of jobs. The tech giant is reportedly planning significant workforce reductions, though exact numbers have not been disclosed. This comes amid efforts to streamline operations and improve efficiency. These developments have caught the attention of investors, contributing to the slight uptick in Oracle's stock.

data.title: Kitsault Energy Continues to Advance Goals to Create West Coast Energy/Commodity Corridor—Increasing Canadian Exports
data.description: In Ottawa, Ontario, the demand for energy is shifting towards the Pacific Rim, prompting Canada to look for new export opportunities beyond the US. While most infrastructure projects face delays, Kitsault Energy (KE) is pursuing a corridor-based strategy to link Western Canadian resources with global markets. Since January 2013, Krishnan Suthanthiran, the President and Founder of KE, has spearheaded this initiative. Canada aims to leverage this solution to tap into international markets and address the growing demand for energy, with significant potential economic benefits.

data.title: America First Credit Union Kicks Off 8th Annual Student Scholarship Program, Applications Open Now
data.description: In Ogden, Utah, America First Credit Union has initiated its eighth annual Charitable Foundation scholarship program, offering $5000 each to 10 deserving students dedicated to community service, with exceptional leadership and academic capabilities.

data.title: Stratasys Files Annual Report on Form 20-F for the Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description: Stratasys Ltd. has officially submitted its annual report on Form 20-F for the fiscal year that concluded on December 31, 2025. This report, including the audited financial statements for 2025, has been filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Interested parties can view the report on the SEC's website, http://www.sec.gov, or access it through Stratasys' investor relations website. The company's stock trades on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SSYS.

data.title: Modular Genetics, Inc. joins New American Industrial Alliance to Reindustrialize America
data.description: Modular Genetics, Inc. (Modular) has recently revealed its partnership with the New American Industrial Alliance (NAIA), a prominent multi-sector trade association in the USA. This collaboration aims to enhance Modular's efforts in constructing and managing cutting-edge biomanufacturing infrastructure across the country. NAIA's focus lies in bolstering America's position as a global leader in technology and industry. Comprising manufacturers, builders, investors, and various stakeholders, NAIA will support Modular in advancing its plans. This strategic move is set to propel Modular's growth and influence, marking a significant milestone in the company's trajectory.

data.title: Herpes Virus Fluidizes Cell Nuclei to Multiply Faster
data.description: A recent study revealed that the herpes simplex virus has the ability to partially liquefy the dense, gel-like substance within human cell nuclei. This process allows the virus to replicate more rapidly. The focus of the research is on the genetic equipment located in the nucleus of each human cell, which is critical for the copying process. This discovery sheds light on the virus's tactics for replication and highlights the intricate ways in which it interacts with host cells. The findings provide valuable insights into the mechanisms behind viral infections and could potentially aid in developing more effective treatment strategies against herpes.
